name: Camino Natural del Tajo and Barrancas de Burujón Loop
name_original: Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña – circular desde Toledo por Guadamur y el Camino Natural del Tajo
description: This difficult 47.4-mile mountain biking loop offers dramatic views of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Tagus River near Toledo.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Toledo is characterized by routes that traverse varied landscapes, including river valleys, historic areas, and natural parks. The region features a mix of terrain, from paths alongside the Tajo River to trails with notable elevation changes. While some areas are relatively flat, many routes incorporate climbs and descents, offering diverse experiences for mountain bikers. The natural features include riverine ecosystems and historical landmarks. Best mountain bike trails around Toledo The most popular mountain bike trail is Camino Natural del Tajo and Barrancas de Burujón Loop, a 47.3 miles (76.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural beauty of the Tajo River and the Barrancas de Burujón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Almonacid – circular desde Toledo por el Camino Natural del Tajo y la ruta de Don Quijote, a difficult 43.2 miles (69.6 km) path. This route combines river views with historical sites, including the Camino Natural del Tajo. Local mountain bikers also love the San Martín Bridge – View of the Palacio de Galiana loop from Toledo, a 42.6 miles (68.6 km) trail leading through historical areas and offering views of landmarks, often completed in about 5 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Toledo is defined by river valleys, historical bridges, and natural parks, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Toledo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Toledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Toledo?
A: Mountain biking around Toledo features a diverse landscape, including river valleys, historic areas, and natural parks. You'll find a mix of terrain, from paths alongside the Tajo River to trails with notable elevation changes, offering varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Toledo?
A: Yes, Toledo offers trails for various skill levels. While many routes incorporate climbs and descents, there are 13 routes classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes available?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022974372/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-barrancas-de-burujon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Barrancas de Burujón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 47.3 miles (76.2 km) and takes approximately 6 hours 20 minutes. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022439215/castillo-de-almonacid-circular-desde-toledo-por-el-camino-natural-del-tajo-y-la-ruta-de-don-quijote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almonacid – circular desde Toledo por el Camino Natural del Tajo y la ruta de Don Quijote&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.2 miles (69.6 km) path combining river views with historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Toledo?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Toledo are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022974372/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-barrancas-de-burujon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Barrancas de Burujón Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17322484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín Bridge – View of the Palacio de Galiana loop from Toledo&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Toledo's trails often pass by significant historical sites. You can find routes that offer views of landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Galiana&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Servando Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic San Martín Bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Bridge – San Martín Bridge loop from Toledo&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for combining cycling with historical sightseeing.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Toledo?
A: The mountain bike routes in Toledo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the combination of natural beauty, river views, and historical elements found along the trails.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Toledo?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike routes available around Toledo, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulties to explore.

Q: Are there any trails with significant elevation changes for a challenging ride?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate climbs and descents. For a challenging ride with substantial elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17322484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín Bridge – View of the Palacio de Galiana loop from Toledo&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1000 meters of elevation gain and loss.

Q: What are some of the natural features I might encounter on the trails?
A: The trails often follow the Tajo River, providing scenic riverine ecosystems. You'll also encounter varied natural parks and landscapes, including the unique Barrancas de Burujón, known for its impressive geological formations.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural scenery with historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Toledo are known for blending natural beauty with historical significance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022439215/castillo-de-almonacid-circular-desde-toledo-por-el-camino-natural-del-tajo-y-la-ruta-de-don-quijote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almonacid – circular desde Toledo por el Camino Natural del Tajo y la ruta de Don Quijote&lt;/a&gt; combines river views with historical castles and the Camino Natural del Tajo.
tips: The old road to Guadalupe linked Toledo with the Montes de Toledo in the south of the province. It runs through a picturesque landscape passing through several towns of great tourist and historical interest, such as Guadamur, which stands out for its castle and is worth visiting.

This fortress is a magnificent, well-preserved example of the fifteenth-century residential castles found in the province of Toledo. Currently inhabited, we can tour its exterior, appreciating the aesthetic result of the influences of Italian art in its architecture.

This section of path that joins the Cambrón and Enebros viewpoints located at the southern end of the ravine path, offers privileged views of the entire ravine, its curious natural formation and the Castrejón reservoir, a whole dream complex.

This wonderful place, declared a Natural Monument, is worth visiting and exploring it completely. With 4 viewpoints connected by a path, you will have beautiful views of the Castrejón reservoir and the Tagus river in each of them, all deserving of a postcard!

The Tagus Nature Trail is a very popular itinerary, especially as it passes through the city of Toledo. It allows you to explore the banks of the Tagus River as it passes through the city, offering a very entertaining and colorful alternative for all types of nature enthusiasts. You will be sharing the path with hikers and runners, so respect their pace so that everyone can enjoy this wonderful route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult for mountain biking. The terrain primarily features comfortable dirt and gravel tracks, with some sections on paved roads that have minimal traffic. While the broader Camino Natural del Tajo can have less maintained sections, this specific loop often utilizes well-maintained paths. Be prepared for some gentle ascents and 'false flats' that require effort.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour data indicates a duration of approximately 6 hours and 20 minutes (22,793 seconds) to complete this 76 km loop. However, some similar circular routes in the area are described as taking around 4 hours at a normal pace, including stops, depending on your speed and how often you pause.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery, especially the dramatic Barrancas de Burujón, with their reddish clay cliffs overlooking the Castrejón reservoir. You'll also pass by Guadamur Castle and enjoy Viewpoints of Las Barrancas and Castrejón Reservoir. Other notable points include sections of the Tagus Natural Trail and the Camino de Guadalupe.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided in the available data. However, as the loop is centered around the Barrancas de Burujón and includes sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo, it's advisable to look for parking areas near the Barrancas de Burujón Natural Monument or in nearby towns like Burujón or Guadamur, which offer convenient access points to the trail network.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: It is highly recommended to undertake this route during autumn, winter, or spring. During summer months, the sun can be intense and shade is limited, making for a more demanding ride. If riding in summer, start early in the day to avoid extreme heat.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Barrancas de Burujón area is a protected Natural Monument and Wildlife Refuge. While specific regulations for dogs on this exact loop are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially near cliff edges.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for accessing the trails within the Barrancas de Burujón Natural Monument or the Camino Natural del Tajo sections for mountain biking. These are generally publicly accessible routes.

faqs.question: What should I know about the Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña?
faqs.answer: The Barrancas de Burujón, also known as Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña, are a designated Natural Monument by the Junta de Castilla la Mancha since 2010 and were declared a Wildlife Refuge in 1995. This protection highlights their ecological and geological importance. When visiting, please respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife or damaging the unique geological formations.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity, especially birdlife. You might spot various bird species, including raptors like vultures and marsh harriers, particularly along the Tagus River and within the protected Barrancas area. The broader Tagus Natural Trail is known for large birds such as Spanish imperial eagles and black storks in certain sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo (39% of the route) and also passes through parts of the Camino de Guadalupe (4% and 2% sections), Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 1 (11%), and the Sendero de la Barranca (4% and 1% sections).

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and the presence of dramatic cliffs at the Barrancas, caution is advised, especially if venturing off marked paths. The area can be very hot in summer with limited shade, so bring plenty of water. Always be mindful of your surroundings and potential falling hazards near cliff edges.

waypoints.name: Viewpoints of Las Barrancas and Castrejón Reservoir
